title: Gingerbread biscuits
subtitle: Proper cookie-cutter gingerbread, firm enough to ice
excerpt: A rolled gingerbread dough that cuts cleanly, holds its shape during baking, and dries firm enough to take icing and decoration. Less sweet than many versions, with a proper bite of ginger.
sourceNotes: Gingerbread biscuits have been made in Britain since the medieval period; spiced gingerbread for cutting into shapes is documented from the 16th century onward. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, includes multiple gingerbread recipes. Hannah Glasse, The Art of Cookery (1747), Project Gutenberg: spiced gingerbread. The crisp, rollable cookie-cutter version became associated with Christmas decoration in the Victorian period.

recipe.batchNotes: Doubles cleanly. The dough also freezes for up to 3 months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough keeps refrigerated for 3 days before rolling. Baked biscuits keep in a tin for 1 week.

glossaryTerms.definition: Biscuit dough that is too soft, too warm, or contains too much sugar will spread in the oven, losing the shape cut from it. For cut biscuits that must hold a shape (gingerbread figures, stamped biscuits), chilling the cut shapes before baking and using a dough with a high flour ratio both reduce spread.

content.text: Gingerbread for cutting shapes needs a dough firm enough to hold the cut edge during baking. Chill the dough before rolling and chill the cut shapes again before they go into the oven; both steps reduce
. Roll to 5 mm: thinner and the biscuits become too crisp and fragile; thicker and they are cake-like.

Mix the flour, bicarbonate of soda, spices, and salt in a large bowl. Rub in the cold butter until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Stir in the sugar. Make a well and add the golden syrup and beaten egg. Mix to a firm dough. If it is crumbly, add a teaspoon of cold water. Form into a disc, wrap in cling film, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 180°C conventional. Line two baking trays with baking paper. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface to 5 mm thick. Cut out shapes with floured cutters and place on the trays. Gather the scraps, re-roll, and cut again. Chill the cut shapes in the refrigerator for 10 minutes before baking. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until golden at the edges; the centres may look slightly soft. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes before moving to a rack: the biscuits firm as they cool.
